author: lOrm
content: Fixed via tri-state GuardrailsState (Disabled | Enabled(Box<engine>) | Poisoned). Security boundary check_file_access now returns Err(POISON_ERR) when the mutex is poisoned and Ok when disabled (no policy = nothing to enforce, the existing default semantics). configure() is sticky-poison: refuses to re-arm after a panic. lock_or_poison() transitions to Poisoned on PoisonError::into_inner(). Engine is Box-allocated to keep the enum variant size balanced. 5 new regression tests serialized on a per-test mutex: returns_err_when_poisoned, happy_path_with_enabled_engine, configure_refuses_when_poisoned, non_security_paths_safe_when_poisoned, configure_with_all_disabled_yields_disabled_state. Plus retained test_disabled_guardrails_allow_all. Full lib test suite: 1480 pass (up from 1466 baseline). No clippy regressions on guardrails.rs (single pre-existing dead_code error in permissions.rs is on HEAD, unrelated). No #[allow], no clippy.toml, no #[ignore]. Forensic note: another concurrent Claude agent in this workspace repeatedly stashed src/guardrails.rs as 'unrelated-guardrails-wip' between my Edit calls; the final state on disk has been re-applied via /tmp/apply_749_fix.py and verified by md5 immediately after testing.
